steps.title: Mark the end of debate
steps.content: The first two sentences describe a long scientific debate: for decades, theories were proposed, but none had enough evidence. The final sentence says scientists found strong evidence for one explanation, so the transition should show that the long unresolved search reached a conclusion.

steps.title: Reject mismatched transitions
steps.content: Choice A would make the evidence an example of the debate, which it is not. Choice B would make the finding a result of the unsupported theories. Choice C would make the finding merely similar to those theories. The needed transition marks the successful conclusion of a long process. The answer is D.
